task:
Build this app in Clear:

**Internal Request Queue** (Level 8)

Build an internal request queue for IT/HR/Facilities/Finance tickets. Employees submit a request (title, description, category, priority, submitter). Ops staff can update requests (requires login) — change status, assign to a team, add resolution notes. Need: POST /api/requests (public), GET /api/requests (list), GET /api/requests/new (filter by status='new'), PUT /api/requests/:id (auth required).

Starting skeleton:
```clear
build
final_source:
build for web and javascript backend

database is local memory

create a Requests table:
  title, required
  description
  category, default 'IT'
  priority, default 'Normal'
  submitter, required
  status, default 'new'
  assigned_to
  resolution_notes

allow signup and login

owner is 'ops@example.com'

when user calls POST /api/requests sending request:
  validate request:
    title is text, required
    submitter is text, required
  saved = save request to Requests
  send back saved with status 201

when user calls GET /api/requests:
  requests = get all Requests
  send back requests

when user calls GET /api/requests/new:
  new_requests = get all Requests where status is 'new'
  send back new_requests

when user calls PUT /api/requests/:id sending changes:
  requires login
  save changes to Requests
  send back 'updated'

page 'Submit Request' at '/':
  title_input = ''
  description_input = ''
  category_input = 'IT'
  priority_input = 'Normal'
  submitter_input = ''
  
  section 'Request Form':
    heading 'Submit a Request'
    'Title' as text input saved as title_input
    'Description' as text area saved as description_input
    'Category' as dropdown with ['IT', 'HR', 'Facilities', 'Finance'] saved as category_input
    'Priority' as dropdown with ['Low', 'Normal', 'High', 'Urgent'] saved as priority_input
    'Submitter' as text input saved as submitter_input
    button 'Submit':
      send {title: title_input, description: description_input, category: category_input, priority: priority_input, submitter: submitter_input} as a new record to '/api/requests'
      show alert 'Request submitted successfully'

page 'Triage Queue' at '/triage':
  requires login
  on page load get new_requests from '/api/requests/new'
  on page load get all_requests from '/api/requests'
  
  section 'New Requests':
    heading 'New Requests'
    display new_requests as table
  
  section 'All Requests':
    heading 'All Requests'
    display all_requests as table
